description: A comprehensive history of United States trade policy from the founding era to the present, organized around the recurring goals of revenue, restriction, and reciprocity.
title: Clashing over Commerce: A History of US Trade Policy

description: An accessible defense of open trade that weighs the economic case for free trade against the political pressures for protection.
title: Free Trade under Fire

description: A focused study of the 1930 Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act, its passage, and its real economic effects during the Great Depression.
title: Peddling Protectionism: Smoot-Hawley and the Great Depression

description: A narrative history of the building of the Erie Canal and how it reshaped commerce, settlement, and the early American economy.
title: Wedding of the Waters: The Erie Canal and the Making of a Great Nation

description: An investigation of the 1975 loss of the freighter Edmund Fitzgerald on Lake Superior and the theories about why she sank.
title: The Wreck of the Edmund Fitzgerald

description: A popular history of the steamboats, crews, and shipping companies that built commerce on the Great Lakes.
title: Steamboats and Sailors of the Great Lakes

description: A history of how lighthouses, navigational aids, and harbor works transformed shipping and commerce on the Great Lakes.
title: Mastering the Inland Seas

description: The story of how the standardized shipping container slashed freight costs and remade global trade and supply chains.
title: The Box: How the Shipping Container Made the World Smaller and the World Economy Bigger

description: An inside account of the negotiation of the North American Free Trade Agreement among Canada, Mexico, and the United States.
title: The Making of NAFTA: How the Deal Was Done

description: A sweeping reinterpretation of United States foreign trade policy from independence through the late twentieth century.
title: Opening America's Market: U.S. Foreign Trade Policy Since 1776

description: An argument that there are limits to how far globalization can go without undermining national democracy and sovereignty.
title: The Globalization Paradox: Democracy and the Future of the World Economy

description: A collection of essays challenging popular misconceptions about trade, competitiveness, and the global economy.
title: Pop Internationalism

description: A classic survey of United States tariff legislation from the early republic through the late nineteenth century.
title: The Tariff History of the United States

description: A history of the binational negotiations and engineering that produced the Saint Lawrence Seaway and reshaped Great Lakes shipping.
subjects: Saint Lawrence Seaway;Great Lakes Region;Shipping;Maritime history
title: Negotiating a River: Canada, the US, and the Creation of the St. Lawrence Seaway

description: A collection of accounts of Great Lakes vessels that disappeared without explanation and the mysteries surrounding their fates.
title: Went Missing: Unsolved Great Lakes Shipwreck Mysteries

description: An environmental and economic history of how Chicago and its hinterland grew together through grain, lumber, and meat trade.
title: Nature's Metropolis: Chicago and the Great West

description: A bicentennial history of the United States Customs Service and its role in collecting revenue and regulating trade.
title: The U.S. Customs Service: A Bicentennial History

description: A political and economic history of the rise, collapse, and revival of global capitalism across the twentieth century.
title: Global Capitalism: Its Fall and Rise in the Twentieth Century

description: A study of the origins and early negotiation of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade and its lasting influence.
subjects: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade;International trade;Trade policy;Commercial policy
title: The Genesis of the GATT

description: A history of the global contest between state control and market forces in the world economy after World War II.
title: The Commanding Heights: The Battle for the World Economy

description: An illustrated history of the engineering and labor that built the nineteenth-century Welland Canals linking Lakes Erie and Ontario.
title: This Great National Object: Building the Nineteenth-Century Welland Canals

description: A narrative history of Great Lakes shipping, from the fur trade and sailing schooners to the great ore and grain freighters.
title: The Long Ships Passing: The Story of the Great Lakes

description: A volume in the long-running survey series examining Canada's foreign and trade policy at a moment of major political change.
title: Canada Among Nations: A Time of Transition

description: A detailed examination of the General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade, covering its negotiating record, policy background, and case law.
subjects: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade;International trade;Trade policy;Commercial policy
title: The Regulation of International Trade: GATT

description: A history of the construction of the fourth Welland Ship Canal and the engineering effort that linked Lake Ontario and Lake Erie for modern freighters.
title: This Colossal Project: Building the Welland Ship Canal, 1913-1932

description: A sweeping history of the global oil industry and its central role in commerce, geopolitics, and the modern world economy.
title: The Prize: The Epic Quest for Oil, Money, and Power
